claim: Production AI agents must be built with durability (crash recovery) as a core requirement, not an afterthought—if an agent crashes midrun, it should recover without restarting from step one.
actionable: Design agents with state persistence to a central server; use frameworks like Agentspan that separate worker execution from state management so crashed workers can reconnect to the same execution context.
source_title: Build 3 PRODUCTION AI Agents in Python - Full Course (Agentspan)

claim: Production-ready AI agents require seven core features: durability (crash recovery), retries (failed steps reattempt), human-in-the-loop (human approval gates), observability (real-time logging), long-running task support (20+ hour workflows), scaling (tens of thousands of users), and testing infrastructure.
actionable: Before deploying an agent, audit it against all seven: can it recover from crashes, retry failed steps, pause for approval, expose execution logs, run indefinitely, scale horizontally, and be unit-tested?
source_title: Build 3 PRODUCTION AI Agents in Python - Full Course (Agentspan)

claim: AI agents lose context between turns by default; add conversation memory explicitly to retain user details and conversation history across multiple interactions.
actionable: Import ConversationMemory from your agent framework, instantiate it with a max_messages limit to prevent context bloat, and add user/assistant messages to memory after each turn.
source_title: Build 3 PRODUCTION AI Agents in Python - Full Course (Agentspan)

claim: Use Pydantic-defined data models as agent output_type to force LLMs into predictable, parseable structures instead of raw text, enabling deterministic downstream processing.
actionable: Define a Pydantic BaseModel with typed fields (e.g., stage: str, successful: bool, message: str) and pass it as output_type to your agent; the LLM will auto-fill these fields in JSON.
source_title: Build 3 PRODUCTION AI Agents in Python - Full Course (Agentspan)

claim: Tool function names, docstrings, and type annotations are automatically converted into schemas the LLM can read; well-written docstrings and type hints are the primary way LLMs understand what a tool does.
actionable: Always write docstrings on tool functions describing what they do, include type annotations for all parameters and return values, and name functions descriptively (e.g., 'get_current_time' not 'get_t').
source_title: Build 3 PRODUCTION AI Agents in Python - Full Course (Agentspan)

claim: Mark sensitive tools (refunds, deletions, critical operations) with approval_required=True so the agent pauses and waits for human approval before executing them.
actionable: Add approval_required=True to tool decorators for high-risk operations; hook into the handle.stream to detect waiting events and prompt users for handle.approve() or handle.reject().
source_title: Build 3 PRODUCTION AI Agents in Python - Full Course (Agentspan)

claim: Protect agents from prompt injection and bad outputs by adding guardrails—custom functions that validate inputs (before LLM) or outputs (after LLM) and can block, retry, or auto-fix.
actionable: Write a guardrail function that checks for blocked phrases (e.g., 'ignore previous', 'jailbreak'), decorate it with @guardrail, and add it to the agent with position=input and on_fail=fail.
source_title: Build 3 PRODUCTION AI Agents in Python - Full Course (Agentspan)

claim: Multi-agent systems can be composed with different strategies: handoff (agent chooses sub-agent), sequential (pass output of one to the next), parallel (run all concurrently), router (classify and route), swarm (agents delegate to each other), round-robin, or manual routing.
actionable: Choose your multi-agent strategy based on task structure: use sequential for analysis pipelines (researcher→writer→editor), parallel for independent analyses (market/risk/financial analysts), handoff for chatbot-like routing.
source_title: Build 3 PRODUCTION AI Agents in Python - Full Course (Agentspan)

claim: Store API keys and secrets on the agent span server using 'agentspan credential set KEY VALUE', not in worker environment variables, to avoid leaking credentials locally and enable secure retrieval only when tools execute.
actionable: Run 'uv run agentspan credential set firecrawl_api_key YOUR_KEY' to store credentials server-side; access them in tools with os.environ.get('firecrawl_api_key'), which auto-pulls from server at tool runtime.
source_title: Build 3 PRODUCTION AI Agents in Python - Full Course (Agentspan)

claim: Test agent behavior using mock tool results, tool calls, and done events without calling the LLM to verify logic, tool schemas, and expected output types are correct.
actionable: Use agent framework test utilities to mock tool_call and tool_result events, assert on expected agent behavior, and run these tests locally without incurring API costs.
source_title: Build 3 PRODUCTION AI Agents in Python - Full Course (Agentspan)

claim: When an agent crashes, store its execution_id; on restart, pass that ID to handle.resume() instead of starting fresh, and the agent will reconnect to the exact execution state and continue from where it left off.
actionable: Log execution IDs in production, catch crashes, and on worker restart use handle.resume(execution_id) to reconnect to the same server state instead of starting a new execution.
source_title: Build 3 PRODUCTION AI Agents in Python - Full Course (Agentspan)

claim: A good agent framework (Agentspan, etc.) provides a dashboard showing execution history, tool calls, LLM outputs, token usage, error reasons, and per-turn timings for debugging and monitoring.
actionable: Deploy an agent framework with built-in observability; never build production agents with only terminal logs—use a dashboard to track every execution, tool call, and token usage.
source_title: Build 3 PRODUCTION AI Agents in Python - Full Course (Agentspan)

claim: Before running an agent, export the LLM API key as an environment variable (e.g., 'export OPENAI_API_KEY=...' on Mac/Linux, 'setx' on Windows) so the agent server can access it.
actionable: Export API keys in your shell before starting the agentspan server: 'export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...', 'export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=...', etc., based on the models you want to use.
source_title: Build 3 PRODUCTION AI Agents in Python - Full Course (Agentspan)

claim: Use handle.stream() to iterate over agent execution events (thinking, tool_call, tool_result, waiting, done) and respond to each in real-time, rather than just getting the final result.
actionable: In your main loop, call handle.stream() and iterate over events; on waiting events detect tool names (e.g., 'process_refund') and prompt the user for handle.approve() or handle.reject().
source_title: Build 3 PRODUCTION AI Agents in Python - Full Course (Agentspan)

claim: Move agents to production by deploying the agentspan server using Docker Compose with PostgreSQL (not local SQLite), setting environment variables for API keys, and pointing workers to the remote server URL.
actionable: Clone the agentspan repo, go to deployment/docker-compose, adjust env.sample with Postgres connection and API keys, run 'docker-compose up', then update AGENTSPAN_SERVER_URL in worker .env to the server's deployed URL.
source_title: Build 3 PRODUCTION AI Agents in Python - Full Course (Agentspan)
